skills.name: devpilot-auto-feature
skills.description: The complete end-to-end workflow for implementing OpenSpec changes with TDD and mandatory code review checkpoints between task chapters.

skills.name: devpilot-clean-code-principles
skills.description: Use when writing, reviewing, or refactoring code and judging quality at the level of naming, function size, comments, error handling, class design, test quality, or code smells. Language-agnostic; defer to language-specific style skills when they conflict.

skills.name: devpilot-confluence-reviewer
skills.description: Review Atlassian Confluence pages and leave comments (both inline and page-level).

skills.name: devpilot-content-creator
skills.description: SEO-optimized blog and content writing skill.

skills.name: devpilot-cv-writer
skills.description: Use when the user wants to create, write, rewrite, polish, or update a CV / résumé / resume — especially for senior or staff software engineering roles. Enforces quantified, impact-led bullets and refuses to paper over missing data with vague language.

skills.name: devpilot-daily-toolkit
skills.description: Personal daily-life toolkit for an Auckland, NZ user — bundles RSS news (世界科技/AI/政治/财经/新西兰), real-time exchange rates, GitHub daily activity reports, and NZ public holidays behind one skill, plus a combined morning-briefing mode that runs all four.

skills.name: devpilot-google-go-style
skills.description: ALWAYS use when working in a Go project — writing new Go code, modifying .go files, reviewing Go PRs, or discussing Go design decisions.

skills.name: devpilot-harness-engineering
skills.description: Use when setting up a repository for autonomous coding agents, deciding what guardrails, context files, or automation to add so agents can ship reliably without constant human review. Covers guides vs sensors, architectural constraints, agent context organization, depth-first decomposition, and golden-principle GC loops.

skills.name: devpilot-issue-triage
skills.description: Use when the user wants to triage, classify, sort, or sweep open GitHub issues to decide what's worth working on. Sits between devpilot-scanning-repos (files issues) and devpilot-resolve-issues (executes fixes). Read-only against GitHub: drafts comments and labels into a local report but never posts.

skills.name: devpilot-learn
skills.description: Summarize any single article, document, or web page into a standalone HTML digest file with reader-mode styling.

skills.name: devpilot-news-digest
skills.description: Fetch and summarize current news into a structured briefing across categories like International, AI, Technology, and Economics.

skills.name: devpilot-pm
skills.description: Product manager skill for market research and feature discovery.

skills.name: devpilot-prd-to-issues
skills.description: Use when the user wants to turn a PRD, spec, design doc, or feature brief into a tree of GitHub issues — every ticket a deliverable slice with explicit parent/blocks/blocked-by relationships and a bounded change size.

skills.name: devpilot-pr-creator
skills.description: Use when the user wants to create or update a pull request or merge request, open a PR/MR, push changes for review, update a PR description, or mark a draft as ready.

skills.name: devpilot-resolve-issues
skills.description: End-to-end loop that works through open GitHub issues one at a time: triage, self-assign, investigate code, render a REAL / FALSE-POSITIVE / NEEDS-HUMAN verdict, and for REAL issues fix in a per-issue git worktree — decompose into 1–3 tasks dispatched as one implementer subagent per task with superpowers:requesting-code-review gating each task, then open a PR with Closes #N. Runs until the issue filter is empty.

skills.name: devpilot-pr-review
skills.description: Review a pull request via two paired passes — an 'Unknown Unknowns' behavior sweep and a code/architecture/testing/requirements/production-readiness checklist — with every per-line finding posted as an inline review comment so the author can act on each one in place.

skills.name: devpilot-product-research
skills.description: Use when the user wants to research market needs, analyze competitors, discover user pain points, or prioritize features for a product.

skills.name: devpilot-prompt-review
skills.description: Review and improve prompts for Claude (system prompts, CLAUDE.md files, SKILL.md files, API prompts, or any LLM instruction text).

skills.name: devpilot-scanning-repos
skills.description: Use when the user asks to scan, audit, or sweep an entire GitHub repository for issues and file them as tickets. Scans security, edge cases, and testing coverage without assuming business logic; dispatches parallel scanner sub-agents, scores findings 0-100, and files each surviving finding as a labeled GitHub issue.

skills.name: devpilot-trello
skills.description: Interact with Trello boards, lists, and cards directly from Claude Code.
